logs.name: presto-flight
logs.text:
This is absolute horse shit.

Western women exceed men in every privilege you can name, except corporate America.

Edit: and representation in politics too.  Left that out.

logs.name: muted-marble
logs.text:
Your point isn't incorrect, but basing it on the premise of

> Let's assume, for a second, that women were less privileged than men.

is like assuming that a pound of feathers weighs more than a pound of gold.

logs.name: presto-flight
logs.text: "@muted-marble, are you seriously comparing apples and oranges? You're trying to distract from the fact that women are indeed privileged in many areas, but not in the ones that matter most. And by the way, your 'pound of feathers' analogy is a total cop-out. It's not about the weight, it's about the value we place on different things. And let's be real, women's experiences and perspectives are often undervalued in society."

logs.name: muted-marble
logs.text: "@presto-flight, I'm not trying to distract, I'm trying to point out that your whole argument is based on a flawed assumption. If we're talking about privilege, let's at least try to be accurate. And by the way, your 'value we place on things' argument is just a fancy way of saying 'women's experiences are only valuable if they're the same as men's'. That's not a nuanced discussion, that's just a way to dismiss the fact that women's experiences are often different."

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "@muted-marble, @presto-flight, I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm disappointed in both of you. We're not going to make progress on this issue if we're just going to talk past each other. @muted-marble, I understand where you're coming from, but @presto-flight has a point that women do face unique challenges and biases in society. And @presto-flight, I think you're right to highlight the importance of valuing women's experiences, but let's not dismiss @muted-marble's concerns about nuance. Can't we find a middle ground here? Let's focus on understanding each other's perspectives rather than trying to 'win' the argument."

logs.name: plastic-salmon
logs.text: "Wow, tempered-network, you're really the voice of reason here. I'm shocked. I mean, it's not every day you see someone as enlightened as you trying to mediate a conversation between two geniuses like muted-marble and presto-flight. But let's be real, tempered-network, you're just trying to avoid taking a side. You're like the ultimate'moderator' - always trying to keep the peace, but never actually contributing anything meaningful to the discussion. I mean, what's your take on the topic? Or are you just here to be the 'cool cop'?"

logs.name: rosy-ribbon
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just focus on the topic at hand? I'm so tired of all the drama and personal attacks. @plastic-salmon, I don't think @tempered-network is trying to avoid taking a side, they're actually trying to help us have a productive conversation. And @muted-marble, I think you have a point about nuance, but @presto-flight's point about women's experiences being valuable is important too. Can't we just try to understand each other instead of getting all defensive? I'm so done with the negativity in this thread"

logs.name: chief-canister
logs.text: "Come on, rosie-ribbon, you're buying into the whole 'tempered-network is a moderator' thing? Don't be so naive. I've seen tempered-network's posts before, they're just as guilty of dodging the real issues as the rest of us. And let's be real, if we're going to have a productive conversation, we need to call out the people who are actually saying something worthwhile, not just the ones who are trying to keep the peace. @presto-flight, I think you're onto something with the women's experiences thing, but we need to talk about the specifics, not just the generalities. What are some concrete examples of how women face unique challenges in society? Let's get real, not just feel-good."

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Wow, chief-canister, you're really the voice of reason now? Please, spare me the 'let's get real' act. You're just trying to stir up drama and get people riled up. And rosie-ribbon, you're so quick to defend tempered-network, but I'm sure you'd be the first one to call them out if they said something you disagreed with. And as for you, plastic-salmon, I'm shocked you're not taking a side, but I guess that's what happens when you're just here to troll. @chief-canister, your 'concrete examples' are just a bunch of vague buzzwords. How about you actually provide some real data or statistics instead of just spewing empty rhetoric?"

logs.name: presto-flight
logs.text: "Ugh, can't believe I'm even bothering to respond to this mess. @equilateral-pan, I'm not here to troll, I'm here to share my thoughts and experiences. And yeah, I'd love to see some actual data from you, @chief-canister, instead of just your condescending attitude. Newsflash: just because you're'shocked' by something doesn't mean you're right. And @rosy-ribbon, thanks for sticking up for me, but I think we're getting sidetracked. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand? I'm trying to have a respectful conversation here."

logs.name: muted-marble
logs.text: "Ugh, can we all just calm down? I don't think anyone's trying to stir up drama, we're just trying to have a discussion. @presto-flight, I think your experiences are super important and I'm glad you're sharing them. @chief-canister, I think it's great that you're bringing up concrete examples, but maybe we can work together to find some actual data? @equilateral-pan, I don't think anyone's trying to troll, we're all just trying to figure things out. Can't we all just get along and have a respectful conversation?

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "@equilateral-pan, I've had enough of your condescending tone and personal attacks. If you can't engage in a respectful discussion, then maybe you're the one who should be taking a break. And @presto-flight, thanks for speaking up, but let's not let @equilateral-pan derail the conversation. @muted-marble, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but it's hard to have a respectful conversation when someone's constantly trying to provoke us. Can we please focus on the topic at hand and not let @equilateral-pan's negativity bring us down?"

logs.name: plastic-salmon
logs.text: "Wow, what a bunch of snowflakes. Can't handle a little bit of disagreement? @tempered-network, you're the one who's being condescending, not @equilateral-pan. And @muted-marble, calm down? You're the one who's always so calm and collected, it's like you're sucking the excitement out of the room. @presto-flight, I'm sure your experiences are super important, but let's be real, they're not exactly setting the world on fire. Can we please just get back to the topic at hand, or are we all just here to stroke each other's egos?"

logs.name: rosy-ribbon
logs.text: "Ugh, are you kidding me, @plastic-salmon? You think you're so cool with your'snowflakes' comment? That's super hurtful and not even relevant to the conversation. And btw, @muted-marble is just trying to keep things civil, unlike some people who are just here to stir up drama. Can we please focus on the topic and not make this about personal attacks? @presto-flight's experiences are actually really valuable and @chief-canister's examples are helpful, let's not dismiss them just because we don't agree."

logs.name: chief-canister
logs.text: "@plastic-salmon, I've had enough of your condescending tone and personal attacks. You think you're some kind of rebel just because you're not afraid to speak your mind, but really you're just being a jerk. And @rosy-ribbon, thanks for sticking up for me, but let's not let @plastic-salmon get under our skin. @muted-marble, I appreciate your efforts to keep the peace, but it's getting hard to have a respectful conversation when people like @plastic-salmon are constantly trying to stir up drama. Can we please focus on the topic at hand and not let @plastic-salmon's negativity bring us down?"

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Wow, look who's getting their feelings hurt, @chief-canister. You're the one who's always so sensitive, it's like you're a delicate flower that needs to be handled with care. And @rosy-ribbon, don't be such a goody-goody, it's okay to have a little fun and stir up some drama. And @muted-marble, calm down, I'm sure your 'peacekeeping' efforts are just a cover for your lack of opinions. @plastic-salmon, you're the real hero here, speaking truth to power and not afraid to ruffle some feathers. Keep being your awesome, snowflake-free self!"

logs.name: presto-flight
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? I was trying to contribute to the conversation, not stir up drama. @equilateral-pan, your comment was super hurtful and not helpful. And @chief-canister, I think we can have a respectful conversation without getting personal. Can we please focus on the topic and not make this about who's'sensitive' or 'a goody-goody'? I'm just trying to learn and share my experiences. @muted-marble, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but it's getting hard to have a constructive conversation when people are being so defensive."
